lines: Okay so tonight it's the Octonauts, the one with the whale.
The Octonauts live in the Octopod, that big white underwater base shaped like an octopus.
Captain Barnacles is the polar bear, he's the captain, and he's very brave.
And Kwazii is the orange kitten pirate, he says "yarr" all the time.
Yeah Kwazii's the one with the eye patch, good memory.
And then there's Peso, the little penguin, he's the medic.
Peso gets scared but he's still really brave, that's the whole thing about Peso.
One day they got a call, a humpback whale was tangled up in an old net.
Why was the net there? People leave junk in the ocean, that's why.
So Captain Barnacles took the Gup-A and zoomed down to help.
Kwazii used his sword to cut the net, snip snip.
And Peso checked the whale to make sure she wasn't hurt.
Then the whale was free and she sang a big whale song, woooo.
Octonauts, let's do this! That's what they say before a mission.
And at the end, Tweak the rabbit fixed up the Gups.
Explore, rescue, protect — okay, lie back down please.
Goodnight, my little Octonaut.
stories.tell_note: Obvious: franchise named aloud twice plus Barnacles, Kwazii, Peso, Octopod, Gup-A all correctly spelled — unmistakable.

lines: You want the Octonauts again? Okay, um, the one with the giant squid.
So you know the crew, right — Captain Barnacles, Kwazii, Peso.
And the Vegimals, the little half-veggie half-animal guys that cook in the kitchen.
Tunip is your favorite Vegimal, I know.
So the alarm goes off in the Octopod, that's their octopus submarine house.
Kwazii's all "yarr, what's the trouble, Captain?"
And it's a giant squid way down deep in the dark part of the ocean.
Deeper than ever, where it's pitch black and super cold.
So they take the Gup-X, the fast one with the wheels.
Peso brings his medic bag in case anybody's hurt.
Then what happened? Oh — the squid grabbed the Gup with a tentacle!
But Kwazii wasn't scared, he just told the squid they were friends.
And the squid let go because Captain Barnacles talked to it nice and calm.
Creature report! At the end they always do a creature report about the animal.
Giant squid, lives in the deep sea, has the biggest eyes of any animal.
Okay that's it, the Octonauts went back home to the Octopod.
Night night, sleep tight.
stories.tell_note: Obvious: 'Octonauts' said aloud, plus Barnacles/Kwazii/Peso/Octopod/Vegimals/Gup-X and the 'creature report' signature.

lines: Lie down, I'll tell you the one with the sea bunnies.
So the captain, the big white bear, he got a message early in the morning.
Something was glowing way out on the reef, all this blue light.
He woke up Kazzy — the orange one, the cat — and the little penguin doctor.
Drink your water first. Okay.
They climbed into the little submarines, the Gups, and shot out of the base.
The base is shaped like a big sea creature with eight arms, you know the one.
Down they went past the coral, deeper and deeper.
And the glowing was sea bunnies! Tiny little slugs, kind of, with little ears.
No they're real, they're called sea bunnies, look it up.
The cat one wanted to grab one but the bear said no, just look, don't touch.
Then the current got strong and swept the penguin away.
But they caught him with the sub's claw, phew.
And the captain played his accordion to keep everybody calm.
Yeah he plays the accordion, the bear captain does.
Then they did the little report about the animal and went home.
The end. Eyes closed.
stories.tell_note: Medium: franchise not named; Kwazii mangled to 'Kazzy', Barnacles only described, but Gups + octopus base + accordion + creature report pin it for a fan.

lines: Okay one more, the one about the spooky fish with the light.
So the crew's all asleep in the big underwater pod thing.
And Pesso, the little penguin in the medic hat, he can't sleep, he's nervous.
Why's he nervous? He's always a little nervous, that's just Pesso.
Then there's a thump on the hull, thump thump, in the dark.
The orange pirate kitten grabs his little sword, ready to fight.
No, no, stay in bed, I'm right here.
They take a Gup out — that's the little sub — to go look.
And out in the black water there's two glowing dots coming closer.
It's an anglerfish! The one with the little fishing-rod light on its head.
The light tricks other fish so the anglerfish can eat them, sneaky.
Pesso was scared but he checked the fish anyway, that's brave.
And it turned out the anglerfish just had a sore fin.
So the penguin fixed it up with a little bandage.
Then they all swam back to the pod and tucked in.
See? Even scary fish just need a little help sometimes.
Goodnight.
stories.tell_note: Medium: franchise unnamed; Peso mangled to 'Pesso', Kwazii only described, but Gup + medic penguin + 'pod' base + creature focus identify it.

lines: Bedtime story. The one with the crab who lost his shell.
So there's this whole little crew that lives at the bottom of the sea.
They've got a round home base and they zip around in tiny submarines.
One of them, Kuwazee, he's got an eye patch and talks like a pirate.
The rest of the crew was busy so Kuwazee went out alone.
And he found a hermit crab with no shell, all soft and worried.
Why no shell? Hermit crabs borrow shells, and his got too small, he grew.
So they swam around the seafloor looking for a new shell to fit him.
Found an old snail shell, too big. Found a bottle cap, too hard.
Then a perfect empty whelk shell, just right, and the crab backed right in.
Kuwazee did a little flip, he was so happy.
Then he wrote down notes about the crab, like a little nature report.
Lie back down, almost done.
And he steered his sub home through the dark water.
The other animals were waiting with a warm bowl of something the little cooks made.
And everybody went to sleep at the bottom of the sea.
The end, close your eyes.
stories.tell_note: Subtle: franchise never named, only one tell — Kwazii badly mangled to 'Kuwazee' with eye-patch + pirate talk + the nature report + tiny subs; a careless reader calls it generic ocean.

lines: Okay, the calm one tonight, the sleepy whale shark.
There's a captain, a big polar bear, leads a little undersea rescue team.
He plays a squeezebox, the accordion, when things get tense.
Don't kick your sister. Okay, go on.
One night the sonar picked up something huge moving slow in the dark.
The captain — Barnnacles is his name — took out the little sub by himself.
And it was a whale shark, the biggest fish in the whole ocean, but gentle.
It only eats teeny tiny things, plankton, even though it's enormous.
The whale shark was sleepy and kept bumping into the coral.
So the bear captain guided it out to open water, real slow and patient.
He hummed along on his squeezebox so the shark would stay calm.
Then he did his little log about the animal, the way he always does.
Why's he write it down? So he remembers every creature he meets.
And he turned the sub around and went back to the round home base.
Hung up his captain's hat and went to bed.
Night, sweetheart. Sleep like a whale shark.
stories.tell_note: Subtle: franchise never named, only one tell — Barnacles mangled to 'Barnnacles', polar-bear captain + accordion + undersea rescue sub + creature log; reads as a gentle original sea tale otherwise.
